en: ...my colleagues, you know, the entire time. Are you kidding with this?
ko: 동료들이랑 계속 같이 있었거든. 이거 장난이야?

en: Uh, so your weekend was a total bust? Uh, no, I got to see Donald Trump waiting for an elevator.
ko: 어, 그러면 주말 완전 망했어? 아니, 도널드 트럼프가 엘리베이터 기다리는 거 봤어.

en: - Hi. - Hey, you're back too. Yeah, um, Chandler, can I talk to you outside for just a second?
ko: 안녕. 어, 너도 돌아왔어. 응, 챈들러, 잠깐만 밖에서 얘기 좀 할 수 있어?

en: Hey, how was your chef thing? Uh, it was awful. Guess some people just don't appreciate really good food.
ko: 어, 요리사 행사 어땠어? 최악이었어. 진짜 맛있는 음식을 못 알아보는 사람들도 있나 봐.

en: Well, maybe it was the kind of food that tasted good at first but then...made everybody vomit and have diarrhea.
ko: 글쎄, 처음엔 맛있었는데 나중에... 다들 토하고 설사하게 만든 음식이었을 수도 있지.

en: Hey, Mr. Bing. That, uh, hotel you stayed at called.
ko: 어, 빙 씨. 묵었던 호텔에서 전화 왔어.

en: Said someone left an eyelash curler in your room.
ko: 누가 방에 속눈썹 뷰러를 놓고 갔대.

en: Yes, that was mine. 'Cause I figured you hooked up with some girl and she left it there.
ko: 응, 그거 내 거야. 난 네가 어떤 여자 만나서 그 여자가 놓고 간 줄 알았는데.

en: Yes, that would have made more sense. You know, I-I don't even feel like I know you anymore, man.
ko: 응, 그게 더 말이 되긴 하지. 있잖아, 너 이제 모르겠다, 진짜.
